name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Memphis, Tn
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit mortgage lender offering character-based home loans with no down payment, no closing costs, and below-market fixed rates to historically underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union as the first housing trust fund initiative, evolving into a national nonprofit organization dedicated to closing the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership. Over its 36-year history, NACA has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 become homeowners, and issued 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments while providing 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally.

NACA's core mortgage product features no down payment requirement, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no consideration of credit scores in underwriting decisions. Instead, they use character-based lending that evaluates applicants' circumstances holistically. Current fixed rates (as of April 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year terms—positioned as below-market rates. Borrowers must complete the Achieve the Dream Event process to become NACA Qualified and access mortgages. The organization also provides housing counseling services and operates member and vendor portals for document submission and status tracking.

NACA distinguishes itself through its mission-driven nonprofit structure focused on equity for underserved populations, particularly low-to-moderate-income households and communities of color. The character-based lending approach removes traditional credit score barriers while still maintaining underwriting standards. They conduct nationwide Achieve the Dream Events for member qualification and actively advocate against predatory lending and corporate landlordism, positioning themselves as advocates rather than purely transactional lenders.

The primary caveat is that NACA operates as a mission-driven organization with capacity constraints—membership requires attending their multi-day Achieve the Dream Events and completing their qualification process, which demands active participation. While their mortgage terms are genuinely competitive, borrowers must qualify through their character-based assessment rather than conventional credit metrics, and availability may be limited compared to traditional lenders.
pros: No down payment required for qualified borrowers
No closing costs, fees, or mortgage insurance charges
Below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026)
Character-based lending ignores credit scores in underwriting decisions
Substantial track record: 75,000 mortgages issued and $20 billion in commitments
Provides comprehensive HUD housing counseling (30% of national HUD volume)
Nonprofit mission focused on closing racial wealth disparity gap
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day Achieve the Dream Events for qualification—not available through online-only process
Character-based underwriting criteria are not publicly detailed, creating uncertainty about approval odds
Limited geographic availability with only select cities hosting events (Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, Hartford)
As a nonprofit with mission constraints, likely has capacity limitations compared to conventional mortgage lenders
No specific information provided about maximum loan amounts, property type restrictions, or income limits
best_for: First-time homebuyers with limited savings or poor credit histories seeking down payment assistance
Low-to-moderate-income households and communities of color historically excluded from traditional lending
Borrowers willing to invest time in the Achieve the Dream Event process for below-market rates and zero closing costs
Applicants who prefer character-based lending evaluation over strict credit score requirements
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers and historically underserved borrowers (particularly low-to-moderate-income households and communities of color) who lack down payment savings or have credit challenges and are willing to attend their Achieve the Dream Event process. The primary caveat is that NACA operates with mission-driven capacity constraints and requires active participation through their qualification events, meaning this is not a quick or purely remote application process like conventional lenders.
services: Character-based mortgage origination with no down payment required
Achieve the Dream Events (multi-day qualification workshops nationwide)
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
Partner lending network for multiple loan product options
NACtivist volunteer coordination and community engagement programs
Predatory lending advocacy and consumer education
Vendor partnerships (real estate agents, home inspectors, settlement agents, contractors)
Below-market rate mortgage commitments with transparent pricing
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
